stepAnswer: Cofunction identities relate trigonometric functions of complementary angles (angles that sum to $$90$$ degrees or $\pi/2$ radians), stating that a function of an angle is equal to the cofunction of its complement. They apply to all angles.
stepTitle: Which of the following best explains the basis for the cofunction identities and when they apply?
answerLatex: Cofunction identities relate trigonometric functions of complementary angles (angles that sum to $$90$$ degrees or $\pi/2$ radians), stating that a function of an angle is equal to the cofunction of its complement. They apply to all angles.
choices: Cofunction identities relate trigonometric functions of complementary angles (angles that sum to $$90$$ degrees or $\pi/2$ radians), stating that a function of an angle is equal to the cofunction of its complement. They apply to all angles.
Cofunction identities are derived from the Pythagorean identity and are used to simplify expressions involving squares of trigonometric functions. They apply only to acute angles.
Cofunction identities describe how trigonometric functions behave when an angle is negated, such as $$\$\sin(-x)$$ $$=$$ $$-\sin(x)\$$$. They apply to angles in the first and fourth quadrants.
Cofunction identities are based on the sum and difference formulas, specifically when one angle is zero, and are used to find exact values of angles.
